pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 80 hardcodes q_name = 'Transportation Method'; task.json description confirms this name; verifier.py references this concept name indirectly
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 71 includes 'Bus' in the list of concepts to create; task.json description explicitly states 'Add 'Bus' to its list of allowed answers'
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 84 sets desired_initial_answers = [uuids['Walking'], uuids['Car']]; these are the answers initialized for Transportation Method before the agent task runs

Web search results confirm OpenMRS has administration interface accessible via /openmrs path. Talk.openmrs.org references 'OpenMRS admin page' and docs reference Administration > Manage Concept Dictionary. Standard OpenMRS installations use /openmrs/admin pattern. Task description mentions 'OpenMRS Administration interface' matching this URL pattern.

Code analysis: setup_bahmni.sh line 14 hardcodes OPENMRS_ADMIN_USERNAME='superman'; task_utils.sh line 13 hardcodes BAHMNI_ADMIN_USERNAME='superman'. Web search 'Bahmni default credentials superman Admin123' confirms 'superman' is the mandatory default super user account for Bahmni/OpenMRS

Code analysis: setup_bahmni.sh line 15 hardcodes OPENMRS_ADMIN_PASSWORD='Admin123'; task_utils.sh line 14 hardcodes BAHMNI_ADMIN_PASSWORD='Admin123'. Web search confirms 'Admin123' is the standard default password for superman account in Bahmni deployments.

privileged_info_summary: The task 'add_concept_answer@1' operates on a Bahmni 1.0 OpenMRS system. It requires adding 'Bus' as a valid answer to the existing 'Transportation Method' concept through the OpenMRS Administration interface (/openmrs/admin). The system authenticates using default Bahmni test credentials (username: superman, password: Admin123). The Transportation Method concept initially contains exactly two answers: 'Walking' and 'Car'. All concept data is synthetic, created programmatically during task setup via Python scripts that interact with the OpenMRS REST API endpoint /ws/rest/v1/concept. The verifier checks that: (1) Bus is added to the answers, (2) existing answers (Walking, Car) are preserved, and (3) the modification occurred during task execution.
